methodology: Municipal product OKRs focused on expansion, benchmarked operating leverage, and adjacent safety validation.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q3 Turn proven waste-enforcement outcomes into repeatable municipal expansion
companyObjectives.statement: Use cross-site benchmarks, executive proof, and adjacent workflow reuse to expand deeper in current accounts.

objectives.title: 2026-Q3 Make renewal and expansion proof reusable across municipalities
objectives.objective: Standardize the executive proof pack and benchmark logic so mature waste deployments can expand to more sites and adjacent safety pilots with less bespoke effort.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Council-level combined value from cleaner spaces, lower cost, and higher recoveries.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Share of mature accounts expanding to more sites or new modules.

keyResults.target: Increase the share of mature accounts expanding their footprint or modules
keyResults.baseline: Expansion still depends too much on bespoke proof packs and account-specific storytelling.
keyResults.statement: Increase expansion rate by standardizing benchmarked executive proof and adjacent safety pilot readiness in the core product experience.

executingTeams.intent: Lead reusable expansion proof and benchmarking.
Lead adjacent-use-case readiness and pilot packaging.

supportingTeams.intent: Support shared governance and case model reuse.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR depends on reuse and cross-account evidence, so it should remain iterative until the proof model stabilizes.

target1.label: 2026-Q4 target
target1.target: Increase the share of mature accounts expanding their footprint or modules
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: expansion rate improves as benchmarked proof and pilot packaging become reusable.

target2.label: 2027-year-end target
target2.target: Increase the share of mature accounts expanding their footprint or modules
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: expansion becomes materially less bespoke across the installed base.
